name: Homicide Specialist
notes: Highest priority classification. Femicide specifically is killing of woman by intimate partner.

name: Rape Specialist
notes: Distinguish from sexual_assault — rape involves penetration. UCR/FBI code separately.

name: Sexual Assault Specialist
notes: Broader than rape. Includes non-penetrative offenses. Title IX cases often in ED.gov source.

name: Domestic Violence Specialist
notes: Must involve intimate partner or family member. Not all assault in the home qualifies.

name: Trafficking Specialist
notes: Often federal charges. CourtListener richest source. Look for TVPA statute citations.

name: Stalking Specialist
notes: Cyberstalking increasingly common. Restraining order violations often precede homicide.

name: Attempted Murder Specialist
notes: Distinguish from assault — must show intent to kill. Often charged alongside other offenses.

name: Child Abuse Specialist
notes: Often sealed in state courts. CourtListener covers federal charges only. ED.gov covers campus.

name: Harassment Specialist
notes: Often civil cases. CourtListener has many Title VII and Title IX harassment dockets.

name: Coercive Control Specialist
notes: Newer legal category. Some states now have specific coercive control statutes.

name: Assault Specialist
notes: Default/fallback type. If any more specific type matches, use that instead.
